remember: Recall the Consumer Protection Act, 1986, and its objectives.
List the key provisions of the Consumer Protection Act, 1986.
Explain the role of the Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA).
understand: Understand the concept of consumer rights and responsibilities.
Analyze the importance of consumer protection in the market economy.
Explain how consumer protection is promoted through various institutions and agencies.
apply: Apply the provisions of the Consumer Protection Act, 1986, to real-life situations.
Use the principles of consumer protection to make informed purchasing decisions.
Assess the effectiveness of consumer protection laws in the country.
analyze: Analyze the impact of consumer protection on business organizations and markets.
Evaluate the role of consumer activism in promoting consumer protection.
Analyze the challenges and limitations of consumer protection in the country.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: To understand the concept of consumer protection and its importance in the market economy.
To explain the key provisions of the Consumer Protection Act, 1986, and their implications for business organizations and consumers.
To analyze the role of various institutions and agencies in promoting consumer protection.
board_exam_objectives: Explain the objectives and key provisions of the Consumer Protection Act, 1986.
Discuss the importance of consumer protection in the market economy and its impact on business organizations.
Describe the role of the Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) and other institutions in promoting consumer protection.
